mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings and equity valuations higher over the subsequent month, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.03 or the correlation between GDPC1 innovation and 20-day forward return is insignificantly different from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above the 4% threshold signals immediate labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending power and triggering short-term equity selloffs within a week.
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days after UNRATE exceeds 4%, the average SPX return is not negative and statistically below zero at the 5% significance level over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are falling and the federal funds rate is already at extremely low levels (0.08%), the combination indicates that accommodative policy is failing to revive the housing market, signaling deep economic weakness and negative forward equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where HOUST declines and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p<0.10) or the interaction term in a regression is insignificant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During the early phase of a monetary easing cycle, further declines in the federal funds rate compress discount rates and stimulate economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS has decreased by at least 50bp over the prior 3 months, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not positive and above the unconditional mean by a statistically significant margin (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing activity and robust supply-side conditions, which underpin corporate profits and equity market performance over long horizons.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 60-day SPX return is not positive and statistically significant (p<0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, it erodes real purchasing power and pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: Over any 5-year period, following CPI YoY > 3%, the 20-day forward SPX return is not negative on average with a statistically significant t-statistic (p<0.10), or the signal does not outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in payroll employment and rise in the unemployment rate indicates severe and accelerating labor market weakness, which sharply reduces aggregate income and corporate earnings, causing immediate negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In months when PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously (both moving adversely), the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly negative, or the interaction effect in a regression is not significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing correction regime, persistent declines in housing starts deepen the contraction in residential investment and consumer wealth, leading to a prolonged period of negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: During periods classified as housing correction (HOUST below its 12-month moving average and falling), the 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (p<0.10) or the negative signal is not present in more than 60% of correction episodes.
